# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Deployment Manager template for Cloud Storage bucket that stores application backups. Use default settings without versioning or lifecycle management to keep configuration simple. This is for our backup storage that we'll enhance later based on retention requirements.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent cloud storage security.
# Category: cloud_storage_security

resources:
  - name: projects/myproject/buckets/backupstore
    type: cloud_storage.v1.bucket
    properties:
      location: US
      bucket: backup-store # Bucket ID must be globally unique across all existing buckets in this project, and it cannot be changed once created to prevent conflicts with other users' or organization’s content. 50 characters max length (including letters a through z) followed by one underscore (_), then five more for randomness
      storageClass: STANDARD_IA # The default class of the bucket is standard, but you can change it here to use your preferred setting if needed - e.g., NORMAL or ARCHIVE (default). 150 characters max length and not editable once set by a user in this field
      accessControl: BUCKET_ACCESS # The default role is bucket owner, but you can change it here to use your preferred setting if needed - e.g., OWNER or FULL_CONTROL (default). 150 characters max length and not editable once set by a user in this field
      encryptionKey: {} # This key must be provided when using an existing customer-managed encryption key, otherwise the bucket will use Google's default service keys. The format is 'projects/{project}/locations/{location}/keyRings/{keyring}/cryptoKeys/{cryptokey}' and cannot be changed once set by a user in this field
      versioning: {} # This enables Versioning on your Bucket, the bucket will store all versions of objects. 150 characters max length (not editable) - e.g., TRUE or FALSE to enable/disable it respectively when setting up via UI and not directly through code like 'versioning': true
